feedback.text: Greetings, I have big interest in video games, starting from history and how they developed to memorable experiences they bring us. And written this, because over time video games (and not only) have become more frequent subject to anti-consumer practices, which both impact our rights and games. One of more harmful practices that started occuring more regularly is destruction of said media, with no refunds to customers and no given ability to use said media, which effectively destroys them forever. I’m certain it’s going to get worse without further action, where current media might be more commonly destroyed. For example games I love: Warframe, Guild Wars 2; in reality they may get shut off at any moment, with no end of life plan in case they end support or company bankrupts, which puts them at risk. To add, from games that are destroyed and aren’t recovered and possibly never will, to include few: Overwatch 1, The Crew, Blue Protocol, Auto Assault, Umamusume: Pretty Derby, DriveClub, Concord. The list goes on at hundreds, and may go even longer, if nothing is going to be done about it. This situation got big attention, including involvement of European Citizens’ Initiative “Stop Destroying Videogames” that aims at issue of game destruction and necessity to preserve video games. And supporting your proposals in this Act, and I would like to make the request to add into consideration customer protections against games being destroyed that were already sold to customers and their preservation. Thank you for reading this.
